ai_strategy_lead.name: Vasileios Maroulas
ai_strategy_lead.title: Associate Vice Chancellor and Director of the AI Tennessee Initiative

documents.title: AI Tennessee Initiative (statewide AI research, education, and workforce strategy)
BT0035 - UT System Board of Trustees Policy on Artificial Intelligence
AI in Higher Education initiative (Provost Office)

ai_strategy.summary: UT Knoxville anchors a statewide AI strategy through the AI Tennessee Initiative (launched September 2022 under Lynne Parker and led since 2024 by Associate Vice Chancellor Vasileios Maroulas), which coordinates AI research, education, and workforce development across Tennessee's economic priorities: nuclear energy, advanced manufacturing, agriculture, healthcare, and mobility. The UT System Board of Trustees adopted a formal AI policy (BT0035) on February 28, 2025 under Tennessee Public Chapter 550 (2024). The Office of the Provost, through Vice Provost Ozlem Kilic, runs a parallel AI in Higher Education initiative with six standing committees covering pedagogy, philosophy, technology, research, policy, and student representation.

key_provisions: Protected University Data cannot be entered into unapproved AI systems
CIO maintains approved AI resources list per IT0001 and IT0002 compliance
Three permitted-use categories faculty must declare: unrestricted, mixed, or prohibited
Faculty must communicate AI expectations via syllabi, assignments, or course materials
Authors bear responsibility for ensuring permitted AI content use
AI detection tools cannot be the sole measure of student misconduct
Unpermitted AI use constitutes academic misconduct
Implementation required by July 1, 2025 under TN Public Chapter 550 (2024)
ai_policy.summary: The UT System adopted BT0035 on February 28, 2025, giving the Knoxville campus a system-wide comprehensive AI framework that balances innovation with academic integrity, data protection, and compliance. Rather than a single university-wide classroom stance, UTK defers AI-in-coursework decisions to faculty discretion via syllabus statements, structured as unrestricted, mixed, or prohibited use — with Writing Center and Provost Office templates widely distributed.

bodies.scope: Statewide AI research, education, workforce coordination led from UTK

governance_structure.summary: UTK runs a multi-layered governance stack: the AI Tennessee Initiative as the statewide strategic office (under research VC Deb Crawford's portfolio, directed by Vasileios Maroulas); six academic AI committees + a student committee under Vice Provost Ozlem Kilic originally convened in January 2023 as the ChatGPT Task Force; and AI TechX as the industry partnership consortium. System-level oversight is provided through the UT Board of Trustees (BT0035) and the Tennessee state AI Advisory Council.

initiatives.title: AI Tennessee Initiative (AITI)
initiatives.description: Statewide initiative launched by UTK in September 2022 to coordinate AI research, education, and workforce development across Tennessee. Led by Vasileios Maroulas (succeeding founding director Lynne Parker, former White House OSTP National AI Initiative Office director). Now ~400 members strong and aligning AI investment with nuclear energy, advanced manufacturing, agriculture, healthcare, and mobility.
initiatives.significance: One of the first statewide, university-led AI initiatives in the country; a model for how a flagship R1 can anchor regional AI ecosystem strategy.

investment.funding_source: University of Tennessee, state appropriation, industry partners
investment.description: Investment not publicly itemized; tied to cluster hires, AI TechX seed awards, and statewide partnerships. Tennessee governor also proposed $50M statewide AI infrastructure investment in 2026 that includes UT-linked activities.

collaborations.description: Core academic and federal-lab partners driving statewide AI coordination.

initiatives.policy_implications: Positions UT as the convening body for Tennessee's AI strategy; creates a template for R1s acting as statewide AI hubs.

initiatives.title: UT Verse AI Assistant
initiatives.description: UT-developed chat-based AI platform launched March 28, 2024, built on GPT-4 via Microsoft Azure OpenAI. Designed for internal university audiences (instruction, business, research) with guardrails for university data. Now part of a UT-wide suite including UTVersal Translator.
initiatives.significance: Shows an R1 choosing an Azure OpenAI-based private deployment rather than adopting ChatGPT Edu — a model for institutions wanting brand control and data governance.

investment.funding_source: Office of Innovative Technologies (UT operating budget)
investment.description: Campus-wide deployment cost not disclosed; built on Azure OpenAI commercial consumption.

collaborations.description: Azure OpenAI Service underlies the platform.

initiatives.policy_implications: Makes a university-controlled LLM the default path for UT-specific queries and data.

initiatives.title: UT AI Hub (multi-model generative AI gateway)
initiatives.description: Secure, centrally managed gateway to ChatGPT, Claude, Grok, and other leading LLMs, announced as a campus-wide offering alongside UT Verse and Microsoft Copilot. Offered by the Office of Innovative Technologies within UT's data classification framework.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates a multi-vendor enterprise AI strategy — rare among R1s — rather than standardizing on a single commercial tool.

investment.description: Centralized procurement of multi-vendor AI access; individual contract values not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Gateway access to multiple commercial LLM vendors.

tools_and_vendors.name: ChatGPT
tools_and_vendors.role: LLM accessed via UT AI Hub

tools_and_vendors.name: Claude
tools_and_vendors.role: LLM accessed via UT AI Hub

tools_and_vendors.name: Grok
tools_and_vendors.role: LLM accessed via UT AI Hub

initiatives.policy_implications: Provides auditable, data-classification-aware access to third-party LLMs rather than relying on uncontrolled consumer accounts.

initiatives.title: UT System Board Policy BT0035 — Policy on Artificial Intelligence
initiatives.description: UT Board of Trustees adopted BT0035 on February 28, 2025 (with implementation required by July 1, 2025 under Tennessee Public Chapter 550 of 2024). Establishes system-wide AI expectations: faculty must declare unrestricted / mixed / prohibited use in syllabi; protected data may not be entered in unapproved systems; CIO maintains approved AI resources list.
initiatives.significance: Among the first state-mandated higher-ed AI policies in the US; sets a compliance precedent for statewide AI governance.

investment.description: Policy and implementation effort; no discrete monetary allocation disclosed.

collaborations.description: Implements statutory mandate from TN Public Chapter 550 (2024).

initiatives.policy_implications: Faculty must make AI use explicit at the course level; data classification gates tool selection. Deferred effective date of January 2, 2028 gives campuses time to align procedures.

initiatives.title: BS in Applied Artificial Intelligence (BSAAI)
initiatives.description: 120-credit-hour Bachelor of Science in Applied Artificial Intelligence approved by Tennessee Higher Education Commission (Spring 2024), housed in the new College of Emerging and Collaborative Studies (CECS). Covers foundational AI concepts, data sources, and tools across disciplines with ethics, social, and legal content. Also offered fully online via Vols Online.
initiatives.significance: Tennessee's first applied AI bachelor's degree at a public R1; sits in a brand-new college designed for transdisciplinary emerging fields.

investment.description: Standalone degree program launch; budget not publicly itemized.

collaborations.description: THEC approval required for new degree.

initiatives.policy_implications: Signals a shift toward cross-disciplinary AI majors rooted in applied, industry-oriented curricula rather than CS-only AI tracks.

initiatives.title: College of Emerging and Collaborative Studies (CECS)
initiatives.description: New college founded July 2023 under founding dean and Vice Provost Ozlem Kilic, offering customizable bachelor's programs in Applied AI, Data Science, and Cybersecurity; stackable certificates; K-12 educator bootcamps; industry training; and the RISE and PATH experiential learning programs. A CECS Incubator and Resident Industry Fellow Program is slated for summer 2026.
initiatives.significance: Structural institutional transformation — UTK created a brand-new college focused on emerging cross-disciplinary fields, with AI at its core.

investment.description: New college standup; total college budget not disclosed.

partners: CECS industry advisory network (~350+ industry partners)
collaborations.description: Industry advisory board grew from 50 to 350+ partners feeding curriculum and experiential learning.

initiatives.policy_implications: Shows that creating a new college can be a faster path to AI-integrated curriculum than retrofitting existing colleges.

initiatives.title: AI TechX Industry-University Consortium
initiatives.description: Applied AI consortium under AI Tennessee, directed by Caleb Knight, that matches UT faculty with industry partners on real problems. Awarded nine $60,000 seed grants in September 2025 for projects spanning manufacturing simulation (ChiAha), additive manufacturing (EWI / One-Off Robotics), livestock disease detection (EnSenSys), pedestrian safety (City of Knoxville / Cubic), geospatial intelligence (Vibrint), sports analytics (Joe Gibbs HPI), cybersecurity (Cisco), quality inspection (Volkswagen), and LLM acceleration.
initiatives.significance: A concrete model for translating AI research into industry deployments at an R1 — explicit 'de-risking' language and production-to-workflow framing.

investment.description: 9 projects x $60K seed funding in Round 1.

collaborations.description: Multi-sector consortium covering computing, manufacturing, agriculture, business, government, health, defense, and transportation.

initiatives.policy_implications: Industry IP and data-sharing frameworks become routine at scale; reinforces land-grant applied mission.

initiatives.title: Foundational AI and Science-Informed AI Cluster Hires
initiatives.description: UT launched two faculty cluster hires: Foundational AI (bridging human and artificial intelligence with a cognitive-neuroscience emphasis) and Science-Informed AI (focused on additive manufacturing and hypersonic defense). Open-rank tenure-track positions at multiple departments including EECS starting August 2025.
initiatives.significance: Cluster hiring in AI is how an R1 converts strategy into faculty headcount — durable investment beyond any single dean.

investment.description: Multi-year faculty start-up package commitments; headcount numbers not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Many cluster positions link to ORNL joint appointments.

initiatives.policy_implications: Research impact will depend on retention and whether cluster hires are embedded cross-departmentally.

initiatives.title: SMART: Socially Responsible AI and Robotics at UT
initiatives.description: Research initiative focused on convergent, socially-responsible AI and robotics education and research in healthcare, education, and transportation, with human-centered design and inclusive methodology.
initiatives.significance: Aligns AI/robotics research with equity and social responsibility rather than pure capability — a growing template at R1s.

investment.funding_source: UT internal with external grants (NSF cited for affiliated projects)
investment.description: Grant amount for the SMART program itself not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Explicit regional relevance and inclusivity emphasis.

initiatives.policy_implications: Trains next generation of AI/robotics researchers with embedded ethics and participatory design.

initiatives.title: Volkswagen / ORNL / UT Innovation Hub and Quality-Inspection AI
initiatives.description: UT, Volkswagen Group of America, and Oak Ridge National Laboratory expanded their research collaboration through 2028 (announced April 2025) with an innovation hub in UT Research Park at Cherokee Farm. Includes an AI TechX project (Sai Swaminathan, EECS) developing real-time quality inspection AI for VW's Chattanooga assembly plant.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates embedded, multi-year industrial AI R&D at an R1 anchored by a Tier-1 manufacturer and a DOE national lab.

investment.funding_source: Volkswagen Group of America; UT; ORNL
investment.description: Multi-year partnership extended through 2028; total value not disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Provides a playbook for embedding AI R&D with major manufacturers; reinforces Innovation Valley.

initiatives.title: ISAAC HPC with H100 GPU Nodes for AI/ML
initiatives.description: The OIT High Performance and Scientific Computing group operates ISAAC, which now includes Dell PowerEdge XE8640 nodes with four NVIDIA H100 80GB GPUs each (full node approximately $166,370; 25% share (1x H100) at $46,965). Researchers purchase fractional node shares; standard 1TB Lustre project storage free, additional at $100/TB.
initiatives.significance: A public R1 that lets researchers buy a fraction of an H100 node is a meaningful democratization of AI compute access.

investment.funding_source: OIT HPSC plus per-node researcher/department purchases
investment.description: Aggregate hardware investment not disclosed; per-node pricing is transparent.

initiatives.policy_implications: Cost transparency + fractional ownership opens access for smaller labs.

initiatives.title: PHIL 246: Ethics and Technology (AI ethics undergraduate course)
initiatives.description: New undergraduate course launched Fall 2025, developed by Adam Cureton, Lindsay Young Professor of Philosophy, with a College of Arts and Sciences grant under the AI in the Classroom initiative. Prepares students to navigate ethical issues with AI platforms.
initiatives.significance: AI ethics moving from elective or add-on to a funded, purpose-built course is an adoption marker for mainstream integration.

investment.funding_source: UTK College of Arts and Sciences
investment.description: College-level course development grant; amount not disclosed.

initiatives.policy_implications: Seeds a generation of students with formal AI ethics foundation.

initiatives.title: UT Verse Week and AI Unplugged Faculty Training
initiatives.description: OIT and the Provost's office run UT Verse Week (June 9-13), a week of 90-minute virtual workshops on UT Verse, prompt writing, Microsoft Copilot, AI ethics, and writing-with-AI for beginner through advanced faculty and staff. Biweekly UT Verse: AI Unplugged vodcast provides ongoing demos, expert interviews, and tool walkthroughs.
initiatives.significance: Institutional investment in sustained, branded AI literacy programming for faculty and staff — not a one-off workshop.

investment.description: Program cost not itemized; staff time and content creation.

initiatives.policy_implications: Formalizes AI literacy as an ongoing faculty development function rather than one-time training.

initiatives.title: Canvas Ignite AI / Khanmigo / Discussion Summaries (LMS-embedded AI)
initiatives.description: Instructure Canvas (branded Online@UT) at UTK now exposes AI features including IgniteAI Search, discussion summaries, translation tools, and Khanmigo teacher tools campus-wide.
initiatives.significance: Faculty get generative AI inside the LMS by default, not as a separate tool choice — a material change to day-to-day instruction.

investment.description: Included in existing Instructure Canvas licensing.

initiatives.policy_implications: Faculty adoption accelerates when AI is ambient in existing tools.

initiatives.title: AI Tennessee Initiative H100 resources in ISAAC NG
initiatives.description: UTK’s OIT HPSC page lists seven Dell servers with 4x NVIDIA H100 SXM GPUs each, ordered with AI Tennessee Initiative funding and available by request for AI research at no cost to researchers.
initiatives.significance: This closes the compute-capacity gap with a named H100 resource and GPU count.

investment.description: Hardware was funded by the AI Tennessee Initiative; no purchase price was disclosed. The page says there is no cost to researchers to access and use the resources.

collaborations.description: UTK ORIED and OIT HPSC internal resource partnership.

initiatives.title: AI TechX seed funding for Tennessee AI solutions
initiatives.description: AI TechX awarded first-round seed funding to nine UTK faculty-led projects aimed at helping Tennessee communities and industry partners adopt AI technologies.
initiatives.significance: This adds local workforce and outcomes-oriented funding evidence.

investment.description: Seed funding amount per project was not disclosed in the public article.

collaborations.description: Faculty-led projects include community and industry partners.

equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Explicit focus on diversity, inclusivity, and regional relevance in AI/robotics research and training targeting Tennessee healthcare, education, transportation challenges.

equity_and_access_initiatives.description: AI-enabled tool that scans course content for accessibility issues and provides alternative formats via the LMS campus-wide.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Title II ADA Digital Accessibility Compliance (effective April 24, 2026)
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: UTK is aligning AI and digital content with the DOJ's April 2024 Title II rule requiring accessible academic course content by April 24, 2026.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: AI Student Committee (January 2024)
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Cross-college student committee (Business, Engineering, Music, Communications, etc.) providing student voice in the AI in Higher Education initiative.

regulatory_context.summary: Tennessee Public Chapter 550 (2024), codified at TN Code 49-7-185, requires the UT Board of Trustees and every other state higher-ed governing board to adopt an AI policy for students, faculty, and staff by July 1, 2025. The UT System responded with BT0035 (adopted February 28, 2025; effective January 2, 2028). Governor Bill Lee established the Tennessee AI Advisory Council in 2024 (co-chaired by F&A Commissioner Jim Bryson and State CIO Kristin Darby). In March 2026 the Governor proposed $50M for statewide AI infrastructure. Tennessee also passed the 2024 ELVIS Act (first US state protecting artists from generative AI misuse) and K-12 AI policy mandates.

narrative_summary: The University of Tennessee-Knoxville is one of the most visibly strategic R1s in AI, distinguished by three simultaneously running strategies. First, a statewide research and workforce strategy through the AI Tennessee Initiative (AITI), launched September 2022 under Lynne Parker and now directed by Associate Vice Chancellor Vasileios Maroulas. AITI is explicitly a statewide convening body, not a single campus center, and aligns AI investment with Tennessee's economic priorities (nuclear, manufacturing, agriculture, health, mobility). Its AI TechX consortium awarded its first round of nine $60K seed grants to UT-industry teams in September 2025, with partners including Volkswagen, Cisco, IBM, EY, DENSO, and the City of Knoxville. Second, a governance and academic strategy led by Vice Provost Ozlem Kilic through the AI in Higher Education initiative, which evolved out of the February 2023 ChatGPT Task Force into six standing faculty committees and a student committee. The UT System Board of Trustees codified statewide guardrails in BT0035 (Feb 28, 2025), implementing the 2024 Tennessee Public Chapter 550 mandate and setting three faculty use levels (unrestricted, mixed, prohibited). Third, an enterprise and academic-programs strategy: OIT under VC Ramon Padilla offers UT Verse (GPT-4 via Azure OpenAI, launched March 2024), UT AI Hub (gateway to ChatGPT/Claude/Grok), and Microsoft Copilot as campus-wide defaults; ISAAC HPC provides fractional H100 node access; and the new College of Emerging and Collaborative Studies (CECS), founded July 2023 under Kilic as dean, houses Tennessee's first applied AI bachelor's degree (approved THEC Spring 2024) plus certificates. Faculty development runs through UT Verse Week, the AI Unplugged vodcast, and Adam Cureton's new PHIL 246 ethics course. Deep ORNL ties (UT-ORII, 80+ joint appointments) and the extended Volkswagen / ORNL / UT research hub anchor the research scale. Remaining gaps: UTK does not have a dedicated CAIO title, public-sector investment totals are largely undisclosed, and student-services predictive-AI deployments are less visible than peer flagships.

gaps_acknowledged: No disclosed dollar value for AI Tennessee Initiative total investment.
No dedicated 'Chief AI Officer' title; AI leadership is distributed across AVC Maroulas (AITI), VP Kilic (Provost/CECS), and VC Padilla (OIT).
No public deployment details for AI in admissions or a dedicated predictive-analytics retention platform at UTK specifically (UT System references general analytics).
No public enrollment number of UT Verse or UT AI Hub users.
No specific endowed AI chair or named AI gift in public sources.
No active AI-related controversy or litigation identified in public sources.
Ramon Padilla's March 2026 transition to Sr. VC for Finance & Administration creates a CIO succession picture that is not yet fully public.
UTK now discloses AI Tennessee Initiative H100 count and no-cost access, but public sources still do not disclose UT Verse or UT AI Hub user counts.
Worker 3 did not find public student subgroup outcome metrics for UT Verse, UT AI Hub, or AI TechX.
